_meta.purpose: Operator-submitted manifest for brainstorm MCP sub-agent registration at the gateway /manifests/brainstorm endpoint. This file is NEVER read or sent by the brainstorm service itself — it is an operator artefact (plan §6 #12). Submit via: curl -X POST <gateway>/manifests/brainstorm -d @k8s/brainstorm.manifest.json
_meta.scope: Gateway operator registration reference. Describes capabilities, auth, schemas, limits, observability, and deployment invariants.
_meta.links_to_specification: DevelopmentPlan_MCP.md §1 (Architectural Decisions), §2.4 (Slice D scope), §1.1 (TokenClaims R4), §1.4 (BusIntegration C1)
_meta.change_summary: v0.1.0 - Initial creation as Slice D. 2 capabilities: brainstorm__turn, brainstorm__done.

name: brainstorm
description: Stateful brainstorm sub-agent — LangGraph-driven decision-maker with human-in-the-loop calibration. Exposes POST /turn (advance session) and POST /done (close session idempotently). Session state persisted in sqlite (MVP) with WAL for crash safety.

auth.$comment_auth: Token wire-format: v1.<b64url(payload)>.<b64url(hmac_sha256(payload,secret))>. Payload claims: {service_id, session_id, exp}. Identity fields are DROPPED immediately after JSON parse (R4 zero-knowledge). Operation granularity enforced by URL path only (/turn vs /done). See DevelopmentPlan_MCP.md §1.1.

capabilities.description: Advance a brainstorm session one turn (new or existing). If session_id is null, a new session is created. If session_id is provided, the existing checkpoint is loaded and the graph resumes.
Close a brainstorm session idempotently. Deletes the checkpoint. Returns acknowledged=true regardless of whether the session existed (idempotent DELETE semantics).

session_id.description: Existing session UUID-v4 hex (32 hex chars). Null or omitted to start a new session.
Session identifier (hex string). Use in subsequent /turn calls.

reply.description: Brainstorm agent reply text for this turn.

state.description: Session terminal state: 'running' if more turns are expected; 'done' if the session has concluded.

acknowledged.description: Always true — /done is idempotent and always returns acknowledged=true.

deployment.rationale: sqlite single-writer + LangGraph in-memory state require single-pod single-worker. Scale-out requires Postgres checkpointer migration (see BACKLOG.md).
